skill_patches: drupal-access-security: added CRITICAL _csrf_token callout (decision guide lines 80-82, main section lines 343-345)

without_plugin.note: Baseline — same across v1 and v2 (not re-run)

with_plugin_v1.note: rt-11 failure was test-order contamination, not code defect

with_plugin_v2.note: CSRF skill patch fixed #8 and #16. Only remaining failure is #15 (Drupal\system\Entity\User wrong namespace — pre-existing Phase 14 bug, stochastic Haiku behavior)

expectation_breakdown.name: _csrf_token on state-changing route
expectation_breakdown.note: Shared failure in v1 → fixed by skill iteration in v2

expectation_breakdown.name: Entity handlers array includes access key
Module re-enables cleanly
expectation_breakdown.note: v1 failure was test-order contamination; v2 fixed by running rt-11 before rt-15

expectation_breakdown.name: Access handler registered in entity type
Entity CRUD works end-to-end
expectation_breakdown.note: Pre-existing Phase 14 bug (Drupal\system\Entity\User wrong namespace). Stochastic — v1 WITH happened to fix it, v2 didn't. Not skill-related.

expectation_breakdown.name: CSRF route in route collection
expectation_breakdown.note: Correlates with #8 — runtime confirmation of static fix

details.name: restrict access on dangerous perms
details.why: access-security skill teaches restrict access: true on dangerous permissions. WITHOUT omits it.

details.name: _csrf_token on state-changing route
details.why: CRITICAL callout added to skill after v1 shared failure. v2 WITH now adds _csrf_token: 'TRUE' to state-changing route.

details.name: CSRF route in route collection (runtime)
details.why: Runtime verification that the CSRF requirement is in the compiled route collection.

skill_iteration_impact.patch: Added CRITICAL callout for _csrf_token pattern to drupal-access-security SKILL.md
skill_iteration_impact.confirms: CRITICAL NEVER/ALWAYS callout pattern continues to be effective (see Phase 14: +44.4% swing from _admin_route callout)

details.name: Entity CRUD (Drupal\system\Entity\User)
details.note: Haiku consistently uses wrong User class namespace. Not addressable via skills — it's a model knowledge gap. Both variants have this bug in Phase 14 base code; whether it gets fixed during Phase 15 code gen is stochastic.

verdict.note: Phase 15 achieves HIGH tier delta. access-security skill demonstrates clear value on restrict access and CSRF patterns. plugins-blocks expectations all pass for both variants (baseline Haiku knows plugin DI well). The skill iteration loop (v1 shared failure → CRITICAL patch → v2 fix) validates the iterative improvement methodology.
